authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(8) — Arroyo Leon (San Mateo Co.)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 233436, "Arroyo Leon", Stream, San Mateo Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":233436,"gaz_name":"Arroyo Leon","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"San Mateo"}; geometry is a multipoint of the digitized flowline's two endpoints (Web Mercator [-13628441.9563, 4504113.8862] and [-13619226.7269, 4501971.2643]), converting to (-122.426378, 37.464946) and (-122.343595, 37.449667) respectively — neither endpoint is a clean single representative point and both sit ~1.7-7.5km from the stream's named-point coordinate, so falling back per convention to the USGS topographic map coordinate cited by Wikipedia's Arroyo Leon infobox (37.44993, -122.41239, sourced to a 2007 TopoZone/USGS quad lookup), which matches this file's recorded coordinate (-122.4120, 37.4500) to within ~40m — coordinate confirmed via that fallback; gaz_id 233436 confirmed as the correct GNIS identifier for this stream by name/county/state match.

parameters.note: Closed to all fishing all year. Arroyo Leon is a tributary of Pilarcitos Creek (Half Moon Bay area), which supports steelhead.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
